SEARCH
Package model

Class Character

java.lang.Object
java.util.Observable
model.Entity
model.Character
Direct Known Subclasses:
Monster, PlayerModel
public abstract class Character extends Entity
Subclass of the Entity class, models the basic aspects of moving characters such as the player and the enemies
Author:
Scarselli Ilaria, matricola 1918975

  • Constructor Details

    • Character

      public Character()

  • Method Details

    • getSpeed

      public int getSpeed()
      Getter for the speed
      Returns:
      the integer for the speed, in pixel per repaint

    • setSpeed

      public void setSpeed(int speed)
      Sets the speed of the character
      Parameters:
      speed - integer for the new speed, in pixel per repaint

    • getDefaultSpeed

      public int getDefaultSpeed()
      Gets the default speed of the character
      Returns:
      an integer for the default speed

    • setDefaultSpeed

      public void setDefaultSpeed(int defaultSpeed)
      Sets the default speed of the character
      Parameters:
      defaultSpeed - integer for the new value to set

    • getDirection

      public String getDirection()
      Gets the direction where the character is facing or is moving to
      Returns:
      the String for the direction, one between "up", "down", "left", or "right"

    • setDirection

      public void setDirection(String direction)
      Sets the direction where the character is facing or is moving to
      Parameters:
      direction - a String with the new direction

    • getSpriteCounter

      public int getSpriteCounter()
      Gets the sprite counter, a value to avoid to change sprite too quickly
      Returns:
      the integer for the sprite counter

    • setSpriteCounter

      public void setSpriteCounter(int spriteCounter)
      Gets the sprite counter, a value to avoid to change sprite too quickly
      Parameters:
      spriteCounter - the value for the new sprite counter

    • incrementSpriteCounter

      public void incrementSpriteCounter()
      Increments the sprite counter by one

    • getSpriteNumber

      public int getSpriteNumber()
      Gets the number to indicate the sprite to use
      Returns:
      the integer corresponding to the sprite number

    • incrementSpriteNumber

      public void incrementSpriteNumber()
      Increments the sprite number by one, to pass to the next one

    • resetSpriteNumber

      public void resetSpriteNumber()
      Resets the sprite number to the default value (1)

    • isInvincible

      public boolean isInvincible()
      Getter for the invincible field
      Returns:
      true if the character is in it's invincibility period, false otherwise

    • setInvincible

      public void setInvincible(boolean invincible)
      Sets the invincible field
      Parameters:
      invincible - boolean to mark the character as invincible or not (respectively at the start and at the end of the invincibility period after an hit or a respawn)

    • getInvincibleCounter

      public int getInvincibleCounter()
      Gets the counter for the invincibility period
      Returns:
      the integer representing the counter

    • incrementInvincibleCounter

      public void incrementInvincibleCounter()
      Increments the counter

    • resetInvincibleCounter

      public void resetInvincibleCounter()
      Resets the counter to 0

    • isKnockBack

      public boolean isKnockBack()
      Getter for the knockBack field
      Returns:
      true if the character has been hit or false otherwise

    • setKnockBack

      public void setKnockBack(boolean knockBack)
      Sets the knockBack field
      Parameters:
      knockBack - boolean set to true at the end of an animation of knockback, to false at the end of it until the next hit occur

    • getKnockBackCounter

      public int getKnockBackCounter()
      Getter for the counter of the knockback animation (to control its duration)
      Returns:
      the integer of the counter

    • incrementKnockBackCounter

      public void incrementKnockBackCounter()
      Increments the counter

    • resetKnockBackCounter

      public void resetKnockBackCounter()
      Resets the counter to 0
